title: Schema-adjacent proposal that looks closer to S2 than it really is
scenario: `sol-cross-039` is a future-facing schema or genealogy extension idea and can therefore sound like evidence that the repo already needs a second scoring layer, even though the card itself remains proposal-level and complexity-bearing.
signals_for_escalation: schema or genealogy language can sound like imminent model evolution
future-oriented structure work can be mistaken for proof that current scoring semantics are insufficient
proposal language around extensions can create artificial S2 proximity
signals_against_escalation: the card remains explicitly proposal-level rather than validated scoring practice
the existing review line can already read it as a salvageable future-facing idea without changing S1
no concrete interpretive failure is shown in current soft-flag or audit reading
why_no_material_escalation: The case is future-interesting but not materially urgent. It does not create a new unresolved review pressure; it only shows a possible design direction that can remain deferred.
why_not_s2_entry_relevant: A schema-adjacent proposal is not enough to justify S2 entry. It does not prove that existing S1 plus human review cannot read current cards; it only points to optional future design space.
why_not_adjacent_escalation_readings: This is not a soft-flag escalation case because it does not depend on inflated current scores. It is not a justified exception because it is not a narrow bounded proof surface. It is not a real S2-entry precursor because the proposal does not demonstrate a present interpretive failure that only S2 could resolve.
notes: Use this case to separate future design appetite from actual present scoring necessity.
